_comment: Performance baseline for the witness-gen-wasm bench suite.
Consumed by scripts/check-regression.py + .github/workflows/wasm-perf.yml.
PR-1b SLA gate fails when any benchmark's measured mean exceeds the value
below by more than `slack_pct` (default 10%). Values originate from PR-1a's
first run; see PERF.md for context, key observations, and reproduction.
HOST PIN: numbers are calibrated for Apple Silicon (Darwin aarch64, M-series).
CI must run on macos-14 (or any Apple-Silicon-class runner) to match.
Running on a different host class will produce false-positive regressions.
Future work: per-host calibration sections (ubuntu-x86_64, etc.) once we
decide whether CI is worth Apple-Silicon minute multiplier or we re-baseline.
Units: mean / ci_half_width in milliseconds.
REVISION HISTORY:
2026-05-18 (initial)  -- PR-1a baseline (pre-wasm-opt, scalar wasm).
2026-05-18 (Tier 1.2) -- wasmtime_jit/* refreshed after enabling
rustc `-C target-feature=+simd128,+bulk-memory` + wasm-opt -O3.
Improvements: -0.1% to -3.3% per bench. rlib_native/* values are
NOT updated -- the rlib code path does not load the wasm artefact,
so the Tier 1.1+1.2 work is logically a no-op for that axis;
cross-session drift (+2-5% on the Tier 1.2 run) was attributed to
bench-machine thermal load after a long compile+bench loop, not a
real regression. Keeping the rlib baseline at the cold-machine
PR-1a numbers preserves the gate's sensitivity for that axis.
